generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components //
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor per axis integrated with gearbox mechanisms for precision motion control in the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, managed through Siemens SINUMERIK 840d sl control system with safety cell restricting operational range to [-90°, 90°]."
]

instance GearboxMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear transmission mechanism providing high-precision motion control for the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, integrated with the permanent magnet synchronous motor for torque amplification and positional accuracy."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance PrimaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft of the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, providing high-precision time-series positional feedback for motion control validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PrimaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the gearbox of the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, used to mitigate backlash and positional uncertainty through redundant positional feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance PrimaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PrimaryPositionEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryPositionEncoder
]

instance MQTTDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "MQTT-based streaming protocol for one-way automated data transmission of time-series sensor measurements from physical components to the digital twin representation, ensuring real-time or near-real-time aggregation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PrimaryEncoderDataTransmitted, SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PositionalFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces of positional measurements (time-series) from both primary and secondary encoders, processed through abstraction mechanisms for motion control validation and performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearTransmissionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model of the cycloidal gear transmission mechanism using SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) to define component-connector perspectives, interfaces, and interconnections for dynamic system analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance MotionControlBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral specification of the robot's first axis motion control logic using MontiArc Automaton or SysML state machines v1.7, representing operational states (e.g., operational, fault, maintenance) and transitions for real-time control validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler, JavaControlEnabler
]

instance DigitalShadowDataRepository : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Contextual data repository aggregating processed sensor measurements and derived metrics from the physical system's digital shadow, supporting multi-perspective integration for diverse stakeholders."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler, AADLModelingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) with Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) enabling multi-layered system modeling (requirements, functional, logical, product) for the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, supporting component-connector perspectives and interface definitions."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLModelingService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation environment for detailed behavioral modeling of dynamic systems including gear transmission dynamics and controller logic, enabling dynamic system response validation and what-if scenario analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulinkSimulationService
]

instance AADLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language) toolchain enabling architecture description for the robot's first axis, supporting component-connector modeling and interface compliance checks across abstraction layers."
    DTDFVocab:enables AADLModelingService
]

instance JavaControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "General-purpose Java implementation for real-time control logic and data processing pipelines, enabling integration with simulation tools and physical control systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlLogicService
]

instance MQTTStreamingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) infrastructure enabling real-time streaming of sensor data from physical components to the digital twin representation with one-way automated data flow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling service providing functional and logical architecture definitions for the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, supporting hierarchical abstraction from requirements to physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:provides PositionalFeedbackDataAnalysis
]

instance SimulinkSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulink-based simulation service enabling dynamic system modeling and validation of the robot's first axis motion control, including gear transmission dynamics and controller behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionControlValidationInsight, FaultDetectionAnalysis
]

instance AADLModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "AADL-based architecture modeling service validating component-connector interfaces and ensuring compliance across multiple abstraction layers for the robot's first axis."
    DTDFVocab:provides InterfaceComplianceInsight
]

instance ControlLogicService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Java-based control logic service implementing real-time motion control algorithms and state machine behavior for the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateAction
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "MQTT-based data streaming service enabling real-time transmission of sensor measurements from physical components to the digital twin representation."
    DTDFVocab:provides DigitalShadowDataRepositoryUpdate
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance PositionalFeedbackDataAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of aggregated positional feedback data from primary and secondary encoders, supporting motion control validation, backlash compensation, and positional uncertainty mitigation."
]

instance MotionControlValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results of the robot's first axis motion control behavior through dynamic system simulations, identifying deviations and performance bottlenecks."
]

instance FaultDetectionAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive fault detection analysis based on behavioral simulations and positional feedback data, enabling early identification of gear wear or motor performance degradation."
]

instance InterfaceComplianceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of component-connector interface compliance across SysML, AADL, and Simulink models, ensuring consistency in the robot's first axis architecture."
]

instance OperationalStateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated state transition management for the robot's first axis based on behavioral model simulations, enabling safe operational mode changes (e.g., operational, fault, maintenance)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DigitalShadowDataRepositoryUpdate : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated update of the digital shadow data repository with processed sensor measurements and derived metrics for real-time system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C18: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, encompassing physical components (motor, gearbox, encoders) and their digital twin representation."
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxMechanism, PrimaryPositionEncoder, SecondaryPositionEncoder
]

instance DigitalTwinModelingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Modeling environment for the digital twin of the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first axis, integrating SysML, Simulink, AADL, and Java-based tools for multi-layered system representation."
    base:contains SysMLModelingEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler, AADLModelingEnabler, JavaControlEnabler, MQTTStreamingEnabler
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents operates within a **one-way, automated data flow paradigm** between the physical system and its digital representation, characteristic of a *digital shadow* rather than a closed-loop Digital Twin. The system relies on **real-time, data-driven representations** where sensor-based measurements are continuously streamed to the digital model. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies or latency requirements, it implies **real-time or near-real-time data ingestion** via protocols like MQTT (mentioned in Section IV.A). The temporal granularity is dictated by the underlying modeling tools (e.g., SysML, Simulink) and simulation methods, which may require **millisecond-level or second-level granularity** for behavioral modeling and system dynamics. However, no explicit time-scale constraints (e.g., 'millisecond-level feedback') are provided beyond the passive data aggregation described in the Digital Shadow Reference Model (Kritzinger et al., 2020)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's orchestration is characterized by a **heterogeneous, tool-integrated architecture** combining multiple modeling notations and simulation tools. Key components include:
  - **Modeling languages**: SysML (for system structure and behavior), SysML IBDs (for component & connector descriptions), and state machines (SysML v1.7).
  - **Simulation tools**: MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods (e.g., [29], [30]), and general-purpose languages (C++, Java).
  - **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)**: AADL, MontiArc (for component & connector modeling), and MontiArc Automaton (for behavioral description).
  - **Data aggregation**: Contextual data traces are aggregated and abstracted for specific purposes, as defined in the Digital Shadow Reference Model (Kritzinger et al., 2020).
  The system lacks explicit mention of a **middleware layer** (e.g., no detailed description of MQTT as a coordination mechanism beyond its use for data streaming), but it relies on **interfaces and connectors** (e.g., SysML ports, AADL interfaces) for component integration. Resource management and system-wide coordination are implied through the integration of these tools but are not explicitly detailed. The architecture does not describe **service-oriented design** or **well-defined APIs**; instead, it emphasizes **interoperability between modeling tools** and **stakeholder perspectives** (e.g., [31])."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** methodology [18, 19] and **SMArDT** [22] frameworks explicitly define phased transitions between these layers, where each stage involves distinct modeling notations:
- **Requirements Layer**: Captures stakeholder needs via structured specifications (e.g., SysML requirements diagrams).
- **Functional Architecture**: Defines system capabilities using **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** [17, 27], **ACME** [23], or **AADL** [24], focusing on **component-connector models** with interfaces and interconnections.
- **Logical Architecture**: Refines functional components into **behavioral models** (e.g., **MontiArc Automaton** [25, 26] or SysML state machines v1.7) and integrates with **simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink** [28]) for dynamic behavior validation.
- **Product/Technical Layer**: Implements physical representations via **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** or domain-specific tools, bridging logical abstractions to executable systems.

Lifecycle governance is implicit in MBSE practices, emphasizing **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** [31] and **toolchain interoperability** across modeling notations. Transition management between phases adheres to **layered decomposition principles**, ensuring consistency via **formalized mappings** between requirements, functions, and physical implementations. No explicit mention of certification frameworks or regulatory compliance stages is provided in the document.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
Model fidelity is ensured through **comparative validation** between **simulated data** and **measured data**, particularly for **friction modeling** and **positional accuracy**. The **sensor arrangement** (primary/secondary encoders) reduces **backlash and positional uncertainty**, improving model validity. While the system distinguishes between **passive digital shadows** (one-way data flow) and **active Digital Twins** (interpretation and processing of data), explicit **formal verification methods** or **cross-tool validation protocols** are not detailed. Quality assurance relies on **consistency checks** across modeling tools and **error detection** via deviations between simulated and measured outputs, though no formalized **uncertainty quantification** or **error correction mechanisms** are described.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly detail the **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system. However, it references **MQTT** as a communication protocol for streaming sensor data from physical systems to digital representations. Beyond this, no information is provided regarding network topology, bandwidth requirements, latency specifications, redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols. The document does not mention OPC UA, RESTful APIs, or other communication protocols, nor does it describe edge-cloud deployment architectures, data path redundancy, or other technical connection specifics."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to **industry standards** and **interoperability frameworks** primarily through:
- **Modeling Standards**: **SysML** [17] (for requirements, functional/logical architecture, and behavioral modeling via state machines), **AADL** [24] (for component-connector models), and **ACME** [23] (for architecture description).
- **Simulation Standards**: **MathWorks Simulink** [28] (for dynamic system modeling) and **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** for implementation, though no **FMI (Functional Mock-up Interface)** or **Modelica** compliance is mentioned.
- **Interoperability Standards**: **ADL-based connectors** (e.g., SysML IBDs) ensure **toolchain integration** across heterogeneous stakeholders [31], but no **OPC UA**, **ROS 2**, or **DDS** protocols are cited.
- **Certification Requirements**: Not addressed; no mention of **ISO/IEC standards** (e.g., ISO 23247 for DTs) or **regulatory compliance** (e.g., IEC 62264 for enterprise integration).
- **Compliance Frameworks**: Implicit adherence to **MBSE best practices** (e.g., **INCOSE** guidelines) but no **formal certification** (e.g., **IEC 61508** for safety-critical systems).
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates **safety-critical physical restrictions** through the **safety cell** of the robotic arm (e.g., MABI Max-100), which defines the **maximum working range** to prevent collisions and ensure operator safety. Data transmission is facilitated via **MQTT** (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) as referenced in [52], though no explicit mention of **TLS encryption** or secure implementation protocols (e.g., MQTT over TLS) is provided in the documents.

No formal **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems** (e.g., OAuth, role-based access control) are described. The system does not explicitly address **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., intrusion detection, anomaly detection) or **risk assessment approaches** (e.g., failure-mode analysis). Additionally, there is no documentation of **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., emergency stop integration, redundant validation) or compliance with **safety-critical standards** (e.g., ISO 13849, IEC 62443) in the provided context. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework ([46], [50]) is referenced for semantic modeling but does not detail privacy or security governance. The absence of explicit **data anonymization** or **consent management** measures further confirms the lack of detailed security and safety considerations in the provided documents."
]
